title: fragaria chiloensis var. calyculata
intro: Fragaria chiloensis var. calyculata, also known as the beach strawberry, is a rare and endangered variety of the strawberry plant native to the Pacific coast of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high content of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Fragaria chiloensis var. calyculata include antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health and immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
